title: Why does Lilith possess Angel like qualities
display_title: Why does Lilith possess Angel like qualities
wikitext:
[]: Why does Lilith possess Angel-like qualities?             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Sun Mar 24, 2013 12:47 pm Adam is the progenitor of Angels, and has the same properties they do (powerful AT Field, S2 engine, etc). Lilith is the progenitor of carbon-based life, but has nothing in common with them: she is composed of exotic matter (according to Rei's spectrograph), possesses the most powerful AT Field on the planet, and looks almost identical to Adam. The difference is that she does not possess an S2 engine (other differences are unknown). Why would Lilith be so dissimilar to human beings? An idea I heard elsewhere was that carbon-based life is an accident produced by free-flowing LCL, and that Lilith was supposed to produce a different form of life not unlike Angels (but with the Fruit of Knowledge instead of Life, whatever that means), but her traumatic entry onto the planet damaged her and rendered her incapable of reproducing correctly. (CI states that this entry was an accident, but Rei's and Kaworu's scenarios in the game imply that Lilith hijacked the ship to acquire the Fruit of Life.) I guess this would sort of make sense symbolically, since some Christian and Gnostic stories claim humans are actually a failed creation or something. How much sense do you think that makes?

[]:             Quote Post by Lavinius » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:17 pm Lilith is an 'Angel' as well, and so are we. Lilith has the Fruit of Knowledge, which produced a single Angel composed of billions of souls- Lilim, made of LCL or organic life. Adam has the Fruit of Life, which produced over a dozen Angels, each evidently composed of a single soul, made of solidified light. But all life on Earth- collectively- is just as much an 'Angel' as Sachiel is.

[]: Re: Why does Lilith possess Angel-like qualities?             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sun Mar 24, 2013 1:46 pm Zenoseiya wrote: Adam is the progenitor of Angels, and has the same properties they do (powerful AT Field, S2 engine, etc). Lilith is the progenitor of carbon-based life, but has nothing in common with them: she is composed of exotic matter (according to Rei's spectrograph), possesses the most powerful AT Field on the planet, and looks almost identical to Adam. The difference is that she does not possess an S2 engine (other differences are unknown). Why would Lilith be so dissimilar to human beings?

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Mon Mar 25, 2013 9:05 am With all due respect, I think you guys are getting a little hung up on the semantics of what it means to be an "angel," and you're not directly addressing the OP's question. Essentially, he's asking why humans are so different from Lilith . If you look at it as a game of "one of these things is not like the other," humans are the odd ones out. Physically they seem nothing like angels, but they also seem nothing like Lilith -- who, herself, seems much more like Adam and the Adam-descended angels. Let's forget the designations "angel" and "human" for a second, and ponder why it is that Lilim don't seem anything like Lilith. I suppose the hand-wavy explanation is because the Fruit of Wisdom just does that. But if that's the answer, then the stronger resemblances between Lilith/Adam/Angels would sort of suggest that the Fruit of Life is the default state, and Wisdom is sort of a corruption -- something that wasn't necessarily part of the blueprint in the first place. (This explanation would also provide a continued allusion to the Garden of Eden mythology). It also implies that Lilith, not Adam, was the original transgressor who started this entire conflict -- again, following pretty closely from her Biblical/Kabbalistic namesake. Now, all of this is purely speculation and armchair theory. It doesn't definitively answer the OP's question. But I think the question is a good one.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Mar 25, 2013 11:30 am A couple of points: First, unless the game is a lot more specific in its details than what we've seen so far, we don't know that Lilith never had an S2. It's possible she wanted a new one in her scenario because the one she had burned out after 4 billion years of passive activity. Second, we should be cautious about fixating too much on PWM, since the designation is pretty much meaningless. All matter has particle and wave properties, so what does Ritsuko's throwaway line mean? The Angels are supposed to be made of something else, but what that is and what it means is up in the air. Last, we know that humans have ATFs. We also know humans are very small compared to the Angels and Seeds. Taking these together we can assume that Seed and Angel capabilities are related to their body size, which makes many of these issues go away. And there's support for this idea elsewhere as well: we know Shinji and Asuka can manifest powerful ATFs while synced with their Evas, for example, and Rei II was in much the same boat. It's no great leap to assume Rei III and Kaworu have the abilities they do because their souls have been hanging out in giant bodies for eons. Once the soul hones its power for that long it can probably manifest its true nature no matter the form, but newbies like the Angels need big bodies to give their ATFs focus. Or something. It's all fanwank, but it sorta works.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Mon Mar 25, 2013 2:21 pm The pilots don't manifest their own AT-Fields. The souls of the mothers do, through the medium of the Eva. They're technological bastardizations of the union of Adam and Lilith, with the child of one taking on, effectively stealing the qualities of the other. The resultant thing has the capabilities of both but for whatever reason (presumably the restraint system in the pylons and their inability to move without power) they can't actually do anything without a second soul guiding them, or perhaps they need the appropriate motivation, such as their child in danger, which is when they are most powerful. When Shinji's AT-Field extended beyond his body during the Zeruel battle, he discorporated. Humans clearly have "small" AT-Fields that can be magnified if they're merged with the Eva and given enough power. It's a very Frankenstein thing, this technological twisting of something ordinary into a monstrous weapon. Lilith is different from human beings for the same reason the Angels are all different and human beings are different from horny toads. The underlying material is the same but the expression is wildly different, because it just is. Given there's nothing in the series to indicate that Eva's psuedocreationism extends to conflict with evolution, the answer to the question at hand is that humans are the way they are because they evolved to fulfill an ecological niche and the seeds are shape the way they are because they were made in the image of a species that was very likely similar to us in morphology, since the humanoid shape would be one of the most efficient for an intelligent, tool-using species. Or the far are pure energy/exist in a quantum state like Adam-Lilith-Rei and created the seeds as a monument to what they were before they reached that state. Or Adam and Lilith are literally angels, supernatural messengers from God because the whole point of Eva proper is stuff that is Literally Magic being corrupted and bastardized by arrogant human science ("science is the power of man") and quantified through psuedoscience technobabble by faithless, closed minded men who believe they are above God and doom themselves through arrogance and pride.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Apr 01, 2013 8:37 pm Bagheera wrote: A couple of points: First, unless the game is a lot more specific in its details than what we've seen so far

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 02, 2013 2:04 am Reichu wrote: Coupled with Lilith's "BRING ME THE FRUIT OF LIFE!!!" schtick, trying to interpret around this seems a bit silly to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 4:38 am Bagheera wrote: But in any event Gendo's talking about the gifts the Seeds gave their descendents, not the gifts they themselves possess.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 02, 2013 4:44 am Reichu wrote: "Ate" means the same thing as "now holds/possesses".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 4:59 am Bagheera wrote: Even so, what he says and what's demonstrated in the show are at odds.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 02, 2013 5:12 am Reichu wrote: Not quite. It is an open question why Lilith possesses Fruit of Life-ish capabilities if she doesn't possess one, and vice versa with Adam, but the show is not inconsistent about this, at least on a metaphysical level. For example, Eva-01's possession of both Fruits is treated as something forbidden. The fusion of Adam and Lilith is also forbidden. There is clearly some kind of primordial prohibition against both fruits being in the same being. It would make sense for this prohibition to be a reflection of the FAR's own feelings, and reflected in the creation of their Seeds.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 5:19 am Kind of feel like you're shifting goal posts on me, considering what I originally responded to was, "First, unless the game is a lot more specific in its details than what we've seen so far, we don't know that Lilith never had an S2." I feel like I addressed that adequately.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 02, 2013 7:30 am Reichu wrote: Kind of feel like you're shifting goal posts on me, considering what I originally responded to was, "First, unless the game is a lot more specific in its details than what we've seen so far, we don't know that Lilith never had an S2." I feel like I addressed that adequately.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Apr 02, 2013 9:10 am The Gendo quote fell under "more detail". It strongly suggests, taken together with other information in the show and games, that Seeds (the original seven, anyway...) couldn't have it both ways. They picked one Fruit or the other, and produced offspring that reflected that choice. You seem to be stuck on the issue of why a lifeform with only one Fruit seems to possess traits that we'd expect only a Seed with both to have, and as a result are buckling against the data telling us that Lilith has and only ever had the Fruit of Knowledge. That's not the right way to go about this, IMO. Addressing the problem should be focused on exactly how Fruits work, and just how do the Seeds have the attributes we see. We've been quite creative in the past, though I've seen you dismiss much of that creativity, as well. (The "PWM" concept partly acts as a workaround for Lilith's lack of an S2, for example, not that it makes any difference to you! ;p ) Ultimately, I suspect that the Fruits of Life and Knowledge simply don't make that much sense; i.e., Anno likes them as a plot and metaphysical device but doesn't like to apply the concept completely. If you want to try to find a fully consistent solution to all of this, though, be my guest. I've probably given up, to be honest, and just accept that they aren't fully consistent.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue Apr 02, 2013 10:27 am Reichu wrote: The Gendo quote fell under "more detail". It strongly suggests, taken together with other information in the show and games, that Seeds (the original seven, anyway...) couldn't have it both ways. They picked one Fruit or the other, and produced offspring that reflected that choice.

[]:             Quote Post by Damaijin » Tue Apr 02, 2013 5:28 pm I think the whole "life emerged from LCL" would better coincide with the real world emergence of life (which began in the primordial oceans of Earth). It would also explain why Earth life is so different from Lilith, but still connected to her. Also, I'm inclined to believe that Lilith had either an active S2 engine or some other kind of energy source that they didn't understand, otherwise there would have been no way her body could have continuously produced that much LCL or regenerated once the Lance of Longinus was removed. Also, I'd like to point out the story of (Bible) Adam and Lilith. They both began as the perfect organism, a hermaphrodite, until God separated them into the first man and woman. Even though He made them into two separate and different beings, Adam and Lilith were still both human. Maybe that logic still applies to NG:E Adam and Lilith. Even though they are different, they're both still Angels, and everything that comes from them are Angels as well. I'd also like to say that Adam's Angels weren't incapable of intelligence, so maybe there weren't a whole bunch of differences between what they might have become and humans. I'm not sure how on-topic I stayed, but there's my input.

[]:             Quote Post by Zenoseiya » Tue Apr 02, 2013 7:59 pm Lilith was inside of a giant space ship with extremely advanced technology. The black moon itself might be responsible for her long life and huge AT field by providing an energy source. As for why Lilith would want a S2, it might be because she's unable to move or survive outside the black moon. An S2 would give her the power to do that. She has to have SOME kind of reason to want an S2, even if not that one.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Apr 03, 2013 2:07 pm Maybe the Fruit of Life doesn't actually refers to immortality and power, then, if Lilith demonstrates it? Like, just as a hypothetical, throwaway idea, maybe the Fruit of Life is the fruit of reality fuckery, something Lilith only demonstrates after absorbing Adam and something we've seem almost all the Angels do to some extent. Maybe the Fruit of Life is the power to define your own form, idk. but if Lilith = Immortal =/= Has the Fruit of Life, then Fruit of Life =/= Immortality.

[]:             Quote Post by K40s » Thu Apr 04, 2013 9:19 am But not all angels are giant beings, what about the one that parasited unit [s:15w7kovl]04[/s:15w7kovl]03? or the one that was a microscopic computer colony-thing that infected the simulation bodies and then hacked into magi? In the NGE2 game, wasn't eva 01 at the end when she got both fruits and served as the vessel for the souls of some humans, considered a new seed of life or a new god?

Lilith is the progenitor of carbon-based life, but has nothing in common with them: she is composed of exotic matter (according to Rei's spectrograph), possesses the most powerful AT Field on the planet,
